formal_verification_specification.title: Checkmate-Escrow Smart Contract - Formal Verification Specification
formal_verification_specification.specification_scope: Complete formal verification specification for code generation, documentation, and security analysis

executive_summary.contract_purpose: Trustless chess betting on Stellar - escrows player stakes and automatically distributes payouts via oracle-verified match results with optional dispute resolution via community voting.

specification_files.description: Entry points 1-20: initialization, pausing, token allowlist, and core match lifecycle (create, deposit, submit_result, cancel, pause, resume, expire)
Entry points 21-40: query functions, admin management, snapshot access, dispute lifecycle, and match collection endpoints
Entry points 41-55: remaining query functions, balance snapshots, match collections, oracle management, and vesting payouts
Complete state machine specification: 6 states (Pending, Active, PendingResult, Completed, Cancelled, Paused), all valid transitions, 6x6 transition matrix
20 critical safety and liveness invariants: no double-payout, no fund loss, no unreachable states, tier validation, authorization boundaries, etc.
20 identified vulnerabilities with severity levels, attack scenarios, root causes, and active mitigations
This file - comprehensive overview, specification structure, and key findings

valid_paths: Pending -> Active -> PendingResult -> Completed (with dispute period)
Pending -> Active -> Paused -> Active -> PendingResult -> Completed (pause/resume cycle)
Pending -> Cancelled (cancel before deposit or timeout)

dispute_integrity: INV-11: Single Vote Per Voter Per Dispute

double_payout_vulnerability_analysis.name: VULN-1: Double-Payout Vulnerability
double_payout_vulnerability_analysis.description: The primary vulnerability identified in the task description - potential for payouts to execute multiple times on the same match result.
double_payout_vulnerability_analysis.attack_vector: If payout logic were separate from result submission, an oracle or malicious actor could trigger payout multiple times.
code_patterns_vulnerable_to_this: Separate result storage and payout execution in different functions
mitigations_in_place: Atomic execution: submit_result (dispute_period=0) BOTH sets state=Completed AND executes payout in single transaction
State guards: finalize_match can only execute when state=PendingResult (post-dispute); once Completed, no further payouts possible
Terminal state: Completed is terminal; no transitions out except claim (which only sets flag, no payout)
execute_payout is private helper, not exposed as public entry point
claim_vested_payout for delayed vesting only sets flag; cannot re-trigger payout
double_payout_vulnerability_analysis.residual_risk: NEGLIGIBLE - Atomic execution and state machine design eliminate this vulnerability

match_struct_fields.immutable_after: Set by oracle (can be overturned by dispute vote)

field_mutation_tracking.descriptor: For code generation and documentation - which fields change in each entry point
field_mutation_tracking.note: See formal-verification-spec-part1.json, part2.json, part3.json for complete field mutation lists per entry point

state_transition_summary.reachability: All non-terminal states have at least one legal transition path to a terminal state
state_transition_summary.deadlock_risk: NONE - no states with zero legal outgoing transitions

recommendations_for_code_generation: Use state_transition_matrix as truth source for transition validation
Generate state machine guards: each entry point should verify current state is in allowed_states array
Generate field mutation trackers: log which fields are mutated by each operation
Generate authorization checks: ensure require_auth() is called before state mutations
Generate invariant checks: validate no double-payout, conservation of funds, etc.
Generate test matrix: create test cases for each valid and invalid transition
recommendations_for_documentation: Use entry_points list to generate API documentation with state preconditions
Use state_machine to generate state machine diagrams and flowcharts
Use invariants to generate security guarantees document
Use vulnerabilities to generate threat model and mitigation document
Cross-reference each entry point to affected invariants and potential vulnerabilities
recommendations_for_security: All 20 identified vulnerabilities are mitigated; no additional patches needed
Monitor for oracle availability (Active state timeout risk)
Consider adding Active->Cancelled timeout path for oracle unavailability (optional enhancement)
Maintain admin controls for emergency pause and dispute period adjustments
Audit token allowlist before enabling enforcement
Regular snapshots and balance audits using balance snapshot functions
